comment_text.sn37.28:1.3: At AN 6.52:5.2, “authority” (or “sovereignty”) is said to be a woman’s ultimate goal. An aristocrat shares the same goal, thus a king wields the power of authority (issariyabala, AN 8.27:1.3).

root_text.sn37.28:1.1: “Ekena ca kho, bhikkhave, balena samannāgato puriso mātugāmaṁ abhibhuyya vattati.
root_text.sn37.28:1.3: Issariyabalena abhibhūtaṁ mātugāmaṁ neva rūpabalaṁ tāyati, na bhogabalaṁ tāyati, na ñātibalaṁ tāyati, na puttabalaṁ tāyati, na sīlabalaṁ tāyatī”ti.

translation_text.sn37.28:1.1: “Mendicants, by means of one power a man lives having mastered a lady.
translation_text.sn37.28:1.2: By means of what one power?
translation_text.sn37.28:1.3: When a lady has been mastered by the power of authority, her powers of attractiveness, wealth, relatives, children, and ethical behavior do not avail her.”
